title: San Marino anchors anti-discrimination protection in its constitution
description: San Marino adopted a constitutional amendment adding protection against discrimination, extending constitutional safeguards to cover sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pete Buttigieg runs as the first openly gay Democratic presidential candidate
description: Pete Buttigieg entered the 2020 Democratic primary race, becoming the first openly gay Democratic presidential candidate in US history and the first to appear in a nationally televised presidential debate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Openly transgender US service members testify before Congress for the first time
description: Five openly transgender members of the US military — Alivia Stehlik, Blake Dremann, Jennifer Peace, Patricia King and Akira Wyatt — gave the first public congressional testimony by out transgender service members.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lori Lightfoot elected as Chicago's first openly gay mayor
description: Voters in Chicago, Illinois elected Lori Lightfoot on 2 April 2019, making her the city's first openly gay mayor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Satya Rhodes-Conway elected as Madison's first openly gay mayor
description: Satya Rhodes-Conway won the mayoral election in Madison, Wisconsin, becoming the city's first openly gay mayor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jane Castor elected as Tampa's first openly gay mayor
description: Jane Castor won the mayoral election in Tampa, Florida, becoming the city's first openly gay mayor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nathan Ivie becomes Utah's first openly gay Republican officeholder
description: Utah County Commissioner Nathan Ivie came out as gay, making him the first openly gay Republican elected official in Utah's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Donald Trump is the first Republican president to acknowledge Pride Month
description: Via a tweet, Donald Trump acknowledged LGBT Pride Month, the first Republican US president to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Five US governors fly the pride flag over state buildings for the first time
description: During Pride Month, the governors of New York, Michigan, Wisconsin, Colorado and California raised the LGBT pride flag over their state capitols or office buildings for the first time.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Transgender pride flag raised over the California state capitol
description: For Trans Day of Remembrance in November 2019, community leader Lauren Pulido raised the transgender pride flag over the California state capitol, reportedly the first time the flag flew over a US state capitol.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mounir Baatour runs as the Arab world's first openly gay presidential candidate
description: Lawyer and LGBT activist Mounir Baatour stood in the run-up to the 2019 Tunisian presidential election, the first openly gay man to seek the presidency in Tunisia and in the Arab world.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Serbian PM Ana Brnabic's partner gives birth while she holds office
description: Milica Djurdjic, partner of Serbian Prime Minister Ana Brnabic, gave birth to a son, believed to make Brnabic the first sitting head of government in a same-sex couple whose partner had a child in office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gianmarco Negri becomes Italy's first openly transgender mayor
description: Gianmarco Negri was elected mayor of Tromello in Lombardy, making him the first openly transgender mayor in Italy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austrian president is the first head of state to address a EuroPride parade
description: In June 2019, Austrian President Alexander Van der Bellen addressed the EuroPride parade in Vienna, the first head of state to speak at a EuroPride event.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Iris Rauskala becomes Austria's first openly LGBT government minister
description: In June 2019, Education, Science and Research Minister Iris Eliisa Rauskala came out as lesbian and said she is married to a woman, making her the first openly LGBT minister in an Austrian government.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Amir Ohana becomes Israel's first openly gay government minister
description: Amir Ohana was appointed to the Israeli cabinet, the first openly gay person to serve as a government minister in Israel.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Meretz elects the first openly gay leader of an Israeli party
description: Nitzan Horowitz defeated incumbent Tamar Zandberg for the leadership of Meretz, making it the first Israeli party to choose an openly gay leader.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Boris Johnson is the first UK prime minister to have attended a Pride parade
description: Boris Johnson took office as UK Prime Minister on 23 July 2019; having joined Pride parades in 2008 and 2010, he was the first British PM known to have attended one.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jim Watson comes out, becoming Ottawa's first openly gay mayor
description: In an op-ed published in the Ottawa Citizen on 17 August 2019, sitting mayor Jim Watson came out as gay, making him Ottawa's first openly gay mayor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Taiga Ishikawa is the first openly gay man elected to Japan's National Diet
description: Taiga Ishikawa won a seat in the 2019 House of Councillors election, becoming the first openly gay man elected to either chamber of Japan's National Diet.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle mark Pride Month, a first for British royals
description: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle publicly celebrated LGBT Pride Month, the first members of the British royal family known to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Anne McClain becomes the first openly LGBT NASA astronaut
description: On 23 August 2019, The New York Times reported a Federal Trade Commission complaint accusing astronaut Anne McClain of improperly accessing financial records from the International Space Station. The coverage outed her, making McClain the first NASA astronaut publicly known to be LGBT.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bernd Moenkebuescher comes out as Germany's first openly gay Catholic priest
description: In February 2019, Catholic priest Bernd Moenkebuescher came out publicly, reported as the first priest in Germany to do so without repercussions from his bishop.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pierre Valkering comes out as the Netherlands' first openly gay Catholic priest
description: Catholic priest Pierre Valkering came out publicly, the first priest in the Netherlands to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First mainstream Indian film about a lesbian relationship is released
description: Ek Ladki Ko Dekha Toh Aisa Laga opened in cinemas, the first mainstream Indian film centered on a lesbian relationship.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly gay character appears in a Marvel Cinematic Universe film
description: In Avengers: Endgame, co-director Joe Russo (credited as Gozie Agbo) played a man mourning a lost partner, the first openly gay character shown in a Marvel Cinematic Universe film.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lucia Lucas sings the first US opera lead by an openly transgender performer
description: Baritone Lucia Lucas, based in Germany, sang the title role in Don Giovanni with the Tulsa Opera, the first openly transgender person to perform a lead role in a standard opera in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rocketman makes Paramount the first major studio to show gay male sex onscreen
description: With the premiere of Rocketman, Paramount became the first major Hollywood studio to depict gay male sex on screen.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Daniel Atwood is ordained as the first openly gay Orthodox rabbi
description: Daniel Atwood was ordained in Jerusalem by rabbi Daniel Landes, becoming the first openly gay Orthodox person to receive rabbinic ordination.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nigeria's first lesbian-focused documentary premieres
description: Under the Rainbow, the first Nigerian documentary centered on lesbian life, premiered; it largely follows Pamela Adie, an out Nigerian lesbian.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Leyna Bloom is the first trans woman of color in a Cannes lead role
description: Leyna Bloom made her feature debut in Port Authority at the Cannes Film Festival, the first time a trans woman of color held a leading role in a film screened in the festival's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Janet Mock signs a first-of-its-kind deal with Netflix
description: Janet Mock signed a three-year agreement giving Netflix exclusive rights to her TV work and a first-look option on her film projects, making her the first openly transgender woman of color with a deal at a major content company.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: North Macedonia holds its first pride march
description: North Macedonia's first gay pride march took place in Skopje, the country's first national pride event.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bosnia and Herzegovina holds its first pride event
description: The first pride event in Bosnia and Herzegovina took place on 9 September 2019 in the capital Sarajevo.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: My Little Pony features its first same-sex couple
description: The Discovery Family series My Little Pony introduced its first same-sex couple, lesbian pair Aunt Holiday and Auntie Lofty, in the episode The Last Crusade.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Broadway's first known onstage same-sex wedding follows a performance of The Prom
description: After a performance of The Prom at the Longacre Theatre, two women married on the stage — the first known onstage wedding on Broadway, and its first known same-sex one.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Univision airs its first gay-led primetime telenovela
description: Univision premiered El corazon nunca se equivoca (The Heart is Never Wrong), the network's first primetime telenovela led by gay characters.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Songs of Praise broadcasts its first gay wedding
description: The BBC program Songs of Praise showed its first gay wedding, the marriage of Jamie Wallace and Ian McDowall at Rutherglen United Reformed Church near Glasgow.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bachelor in Paradise features its first same-sex romance
description: The US reality series Bachelor in Paradise included a same-sex romance for the first time.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lil Nas X comes out while holding a number-one record
description: Rapper Lil Nas X came out as gay, the first artist to do so while topping the charts; he later became the first openly gay man nominated at the Country Music Association Awards.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Albert Nabonibo comes out as Rwanda's first openly gay gospel singer
description: Gospel singer Albert Nabonibo came out as gay, the first Rwandan gospel artist to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Billy Porter wins a lead-acting Emmy, a first for an openly gay Black man
description: Billy Porter won the Primetime Emmy for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series for Pose, the first openly gay Black man nominated for and awarded a lead acting Emmy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lilly Singh becomes the first openly bisexual late-night host
description: A Little Late with Lilly Singh premiered on 16 September 2019, making Singh the first late-night television host publicly identifying as bisexual.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Meghan Stabler joins Planned Parenthood's board as its first openly trans member
description: Meghan Stabler became the first openly transgender member of Planned Parenthood's National Board of Directors; The Advocate named her among its 2019 Champions of Pride.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Indya Moore is the first openly trans person on the cover of US Elle
description: Indya Moore appeared on the cover of the US edition of Elle magazine, the first openly transgender person featured there.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Zach Barack is the first openly transgender actor in a Marvel film
description: Zach Barack played a classmate of Peter Parker in Spider-Man: Far From Home, becoming the first openly transgender actor to appear in a Marvel film.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Laverne Cox is the first openly trans woman on a British Vogue cover
description: Guest editor Meghan, Duchess of Sussex chose Laverne Cox as one of fifteen women on the September 2019 cover of British Vogue, making Cox the first openly transgender woman to appear on it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Victoria's Secret hires its first openly transgender model
description: In August 2019, Victoria's Secret hired Valentina Sampaio, the brand's first openly transgender model.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: MJ Rodriguez wins a television Best Actress prize at the Imagen Awards
description: MJ Rodriguez took the Best Actress - Television award at the Imagen Awards, the first openly transgender woman to win in that category.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chanel hires its first openly transgender model
description: Teddy Quinlivan became the first openly transgender model hired by the French fashion house Chanel.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
